page: Vultr vx1-g-64c-256g is a general-purpose server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 64 vCPUs mapped to 32 physical AMD EPYC cores with 2 threads per core, operating at 2.0 GHz on a shared CPU allocation model. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is limited to a 1 GB network-attached volume, and the instance includes 1 complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU hardware is present on this instance.

The AMD EPYC processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX, AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, AVX_VNNI, AVX512_BF16, and cryptography standards like AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier performance in single-core stress-ng tests, PassMark CPU scores, and PassMark CPU physics tests. Multi-core performance is strong, ranking in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with large-block read and write speeds placing in the top 10%, while small-block cached reads rank in the top 25%.

Due to the shared CPU allocation and minimal 1 GB network storage, this instance is best suited for workloads that require high memory capacity and parallel processing but do not demand sustained, dedicated CPU pinning or large local disk I/O. It excels in memory-intensive tasks, database operations, and text processing or decompression tasks, as evidenced by top-tier bzip3 decompression and gzip compression speeds. It also supports moderate LLM inference, achieving top-tier speeds on small models and strong performance on medium 7B models.
description: Vultr vx1-g-64c-256g is a general-purpose server in the VX1 Cloud Compute family, configured with 64 vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an AMD EPYC processor with 32 physical cores and a shared CPU allocation, it operates at 2.0 GHz and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AES cryptography. The server features a 1 GB network-attached storage volume and lacks a dedicated GPU. In benchmark evaluations, it delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and excellent database operation speeds. However, the shared CPU model and minimal local storage represent key architectural tradeoffs. This instance is highly effective for memory-heavy workloads, database operations, data decompression, and small-to-medium scale language model inference.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-64c-256g is a general-purpose server featuring 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256.0 GB of RAM, and 1 GB of network storage. It delivers top-tier single-core performance and high memory bandwidth for database workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-g-64c-256g is a general-purpose server with 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256.0 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-core and database benchmark performance.
tagline: A high-capacity general-purpose instance featuring sixty-four virtual cores and substantial memory for parallel processing and database workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM on a shared AMD EPYC architecture
Top-tier single-core CPU and database benchmark performance
High-speed memory bandwidth for large-block read and write operations
Equipped with 1 GB network storage and 1 public IPv4 address
Suited for database operations, data decompression, and medium LLM inference
